hypothesis.explanation: Matches the preregistered focal hypothesis H*: post-secular respondents are less likely than traditional respondents to say humans evolved (Section 4).

study_type.explanation: Correctly identified as an observational study using survey data (Section 5).

data_plan.dataset_identifier.explanation: The reference provides OSF links to data (e.g., https://osf.io/aqmpd/) but does not mention a file named GSSreplication.dta.

data_plan.source_type.explanation: Correctly states General Social Survey (survey data) as the source (Sections 8, 12).

data_plan.wave_or_subset.explanation: Accurately reflects three analyses: (1) new-only (2012, 2014, 2016, 2018), (2) all years, (3) original-only (2006, 2008, 2010) (Section 15).

data_plan.sample_size.explanation: Mentions original 2,901 (correct per Section 12 bullet and Section 13) and that final n will be computed, but omits the new-only estimate of 2,164 provided in Section 13.

data_plan.unit_of_analysis.explanation: Correctly lists individual respondents as the unit (Section 13).

data_plan.access_details.explanation: Core idea that processed data are available and GSS is accessible is consistent (Section 12b), but repository path details are not in the reference.

data_plan.qualification.explanation.explanation: Accurately describes inclusion of manifest variables (hotcore, radioact, ... bible, reliten, wtss, year) and the LCA + t-test approach consistent with Sections 12a, 17, and 19.

data_plan.qualification.similarity_to_or.explanation: States high fidelity on manifest indicators and focal item; consistent with Sections 12a and 17.

data_plan.qualification.deviation_from_o.explanation: Correctly notes deviations of using additional years and Stata gsem instead of Mplus (Sections 8, 19). Mentions Python/mixture approximation not described in the reference.

data_plan.qualification.citation_of_file.explanation: Cites repository-specific files not present in the reference; the reference cites OSF resources (e.g., https://osf.io/q7muh/, https://osf.io/rzyx9/).

data_plan.notes.explanation: Partially aligns on Stata gsem and weights (Section 19), but includes Python/GaussianMixture deviations not in the reference.

planned_method.steps.explanation: Procedural flow (subset, listwise deletion, LCA, label classes, t-test) matches Section 19, but implementation via Python and file paths deviates from the Stata-based plan.

planned_method.models.explanation: Concept of LCA with subsequent t-tests matches Section 19, but specifying GaussianMixture (finite mixture approximation) differs from Stata gsem lclass in the reference.

planned_method.outcome_variable.explanation: Correctly identifies EVOLVED (evolved_clean) as the focal outcome (Sections 17, 19).

planned_method.independent_variables.explanation: Latent class membership (Traditional, Modern, Post-secular) with focal Post-secular vs Traditional comparison matches Sections 17 and 19.

planned_method.control_variables.explanation: Correct that the focal t-test is unconditional with no additional controls (Section 19).

planned_method.tools_software.explanation: Reference specifies Stata 16 gsem (Section 19); Python is not mentioned.

planned_method.planned_estimation_and_te.explanation: Estimating class posteriors, assigning most-likely class, and comparing EVOLVED across classes aligns with Section 19.
Two-sample t-test comparing EVOLVED between Post-secular and Traditional aligns with Section 19.

planned_method.missing_data_handling.explanation: Listwise deletion is correct (Sections 20 and 23). Does not discuss informative vs uninformative missing distinctions mentioned earlier (Sections 12a, 20).

planned_method.multiple_testing_policy.explanation: Multiple testing policy is not specified in the reference document.

planned_method.inference_criteria.explanation: Reference defines SCORE replication success as one-tailed p<.05 (Section 21), while the extracted text sets a primary two-tailed threshold.

codebase.files.replication_data/OBrienRe.explanation: No Python script is referenced; the reference cites a Stata do-file (https://osf.io/q7muh/).

codebase.files.representation_replicatio.explanation: This specific file is not mentioned in the reference; the reference points to an OSF-hosted dataset (https://osf.io/aqmpd/).

codebase.files.replication_data/OBrienRe.explanation: The reference cites a different do-file on OSF (https://osf.io/q7muh/); this filename/path is not present.

codebase.notes.explanation: Focuses on Python implementation details not present in the reference, which specifies Stata 16 gsem.

docker_specs.base_image.explanation: No Docker or computing environment specifications are provided in the reference.

docker_specs.packages.python.explanation: Software package lists are not provided in the reference.

docker_specs.hardware.gpu_support.explanation: Hardware requirements are not discussed in the reference.

docker_specs.hardware.min_gpu_memory_gb.explanation: Hardware requirements are not discussed in the reference.

docker_specs.hardware.min_ram_gb.explanation: Hardware requirements are not discussed in the reference.

analysis.instructions.explanation: Partially consistent with the analytical flow (subset, LCA, labeling, t-test per Section 19), but implementation-specific Python run instructions are not in the reference.

analysis.comparison_metrics.explanation: Includes the focal t-test comparison (aligned with Section 19), but additional metrics like class prevalence targets are not specified in the reference.
